name: Dapr (Distributed Application Runtime)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); CNCF project; no licensing costs

auth.notes: mTLS between sidecars; service-to-service auth via Dapr identity

performance.notes: Sidecar adds ~1-2ms per request; negligible for most microservice workloads

agent_readiness.notes: CNCF sidecar-based distributed systems runtime. Building blocks: service invocation, pub/sub, state management, secret stores, bindings, actors, workflows. Language-agnostic HTTP/gRPC API. Components for 70+ services (Redis, Kafka, Cosmos, etc.). Removes cloud-provider lock-in from microservices. SDKs for Go, Python, .NET, Java, JS. Popular in Azure but cloud-agnostic.

gotchas: Sidecar overhead adds memory footprint (~25MB per service)
Complex local development setup (Dapr CLI required)
Actor model has subtle state management requirements
Learning curve for teams new to distributed systems building blocks
best_when: Microservices on Kubernetes needing cloud-agnostic pub/sub, state, secrets
Polyglot microservices that need standardized distributed system primitives
Teams migrating from cloud-specific services to portable building blocks
Actor-based stateful applications needing distributed coordination
avoid_when: Simple monolith or small number of services (over-engineering)
Non-Kubernetes environments (works but less operational benefit)
summary: Dapr is a CNCF distributed application runtime providing cloud-agnostic building blocks for microservices: service invocation, pub/sub, state management, secret stores, actors, and workflows. Language-agnostic HTTP/gRPC API. Connects to 70+ backends (Redis, Kafka, Cosmos). Removes cloud lock-in. Best for polyglot Kubernetes microservices needing standardized distributed system primitives.
